# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Test External Contributions

on:
  pull_request_target:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          repository: ${{ github.event.repository.full_name }}

      - name: Setup Python Environment
        run: |
          python3 -m venv .venv

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: |
          source .venv/bin/activate
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run Tests
        env:
          CC: gcc-10
          CXX: g++-10
        run: |
          source .venv/bin/activate
          python -m unittest discover -s tests/

      - name: Post Results as Comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            if (github.context.eventName == 'pull_request') {
              var result = github.context.payload.action;
              if (result != null) {
                var message = "Test Result: ";
                if (result == 'success') {
                  message += ":green_circle:";
                } else if (result == 'failure') {
                  message += ":red_circle:";
                }
                github.issues.createComment({
                  owner: context.repo.owner,
                  repo: context.repo.name,
                  issue_number: github.context.issue.number,
                  body: message
                });
              }
            }
